decisions.title: Approve FIPS encryption rollout for AWS infrastructure
decisions.context: Multiple FIPS encryption tasks ready (S3, EBS, Keycloak). Required for FedRAMP/government compliance. Blocks security architecture sign-off.

arguments.for: Unblocks FedRAMP pathway, demonstrates security commitment, enables government customer expansion
arguments.against: Performance overhead concerns, complexity in key management, potential compatibility issues with legacy systems

decisions.title: Prioritize Canvas status updates architecture (queue vs poll)
decisions.context: Canvas real-time experience depends on status update mechanism. Impacts user perception of responsiveness and system scalability.

arguments.for: Queueing provides better scalability and reliability, more consistent with event-driven architecture
arguments.against: Polling is simpler to implement, lower upfront complexity, faster to market

decisions.title: Commit to Python SDK data import interface design
decisions.context: Four related tasks (FD-68, FD-43, FD-99, FD-6) blocked on high-level interface decisions. Critical for Sift Edge and Python Rules adoption.
decisions.blockedBy: Exception handling and warning pattern decisions (FD-102, FD-100)

arguments.for: Streamlined developer experience drives adoption, consistent with 'Embed Deeper' pillar
arguments.against: Risk of premature API commitment before user validation, technical debt if redesign needed

decisions.title: Approve Checkly for E2E external integration testing
decisions.context: Evaluation complete (ENG-9585). Need decision on standardizing external testing tool. Impacts quality assurance for Explore V2 and Canvas.

arguments.for: Specialized tooling improves external integration reliability, reduces production incidents
arguments.against: Additional tool increases stack complexity, licensing costs, team training overhead

decisions.title: Security compliance documentation sprint scope
decisions.context: Six documentation tasks ready (security arch, DR plan, incident response, change mgmt, break-glass, DNSSEC). Must sequence for April delivery.

arguments.for: Parallel execution with dedicated resources completes by end of April, unblocks enterprise deals
arguments.against: Sequential approach with quality reviews reduces risk of compliance gaps but delays 2-3 weeks

timeAudit.weekSummary: Heavy security compliance coordination week. Three critical architecture decisions need resolution by Friday. Python SDK design sessions consuming significant focus time.
alerts: Canvas status update decision overdue - blocking 2 engineers
Python SDK interface design blocked on exception pattern - 4 tasks waiting
Security documentation sprint needs resource allocation by Thursday
Only 20% focus time this week - consider consolidating decision meetings
